Ad Text:
Lead Enterprise Asset Management Transformation with IBM Maximo 

Join Transgrid and play a pivotal role in shaping the future of enterprise asset management. As a Functional Consultant - Maximo, you will combine hands-on technical expertise with functional consulting to design, configure, and optimise the IBM Maximo Application Suite (MAS). Your work will directly improve operational efficiency, system reliability, and enterprise outcomes that impact millions of Australians. 

About the role: 

In this role, you will collaborate closely with business stakeholders, IT teams, and vendors to understand operational requirements and translate them into fit-for-purpose Maximo solutions. You will lead the configuration and enhancement of core Maximo modules, including Enterprise Asset Management, Inventory Management, Workforce Management, and Maximo Mobile, ensuring they align with business needs and industry best practices. 

Your responsibilities will span solution design, implementation, system testing, go-live support, integrations, and continuous improvement. You will work with Automation Scripts, Workflows, Maximo Integration Framework (MIF), web services, and BIRT/Cognos reporting to deliver scalable and reliable solutions. 

You will also create and deliver training programs, user guides, and adoption strategies, empowering stakeholders to get maximum value from the system. By collaborating across IT, business units, AMS providers, and vendors, you will support upgrades, performance tuning, and operational optimisation, while maintaining compliance and alignment with enterprise standards. 

What we are looking for: 

Proven experience in Maximo functional and technical configuration across EAM, Inventory, Workforce Management, and Mobility. 

Strong technical skills including Automation Scripts, Workflows, MIF, SQL/PL-SQL, Python/Jython, BIRT/Cognos, system integrations, and performance optimisation.  

Hands-on experience troubleshooting complex Maximo issues, managing enhancements, and providing advice and support to Level 2 Support Teams. 

Strong analytical, problem-solving, and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to translate business requirements into scalable technical solutions. 

Bachelor’s degree in IT, Computer Science, Engineering, or related field. Maximo certification is highly desirable. PMP or PRINCE2 certification is a plus. 

Why Transgrid: 

Drive enterprise-wide Maximo initiatives and asset management strategy. 

Flexible hybrid working arrangements and strong professional development support.

Flexible hybrid working arrangements (3 days in the office) 
Opportunity to contribute to an impactful greenfield project 
Should be an Australian citizen, as you’ll be working on Federal Government projects. 
This role offers market aligned remuneration 
 
We’re seeking a Natural Language IVR / Conversational AI Specialist to design and optimise voice experiences for natural customer interactions. You’ll work across speech recognition, conversational design, and NLU model development using platforms like Omilia, Nuance, Microsoft, and Google Dialogflow. You’ll build conversational flows, enhance speech‑to‑text accuracy, analyse transcription data, and integrate AI‑driven IVR into contact centre environments. Ideal for someone who understands real‑time speech capture and interpretation and can turn it into efficient, human‑like experiences. 
 
Essential 

Experience designing or optimising Natural Language IVR systems 
Strong understanding of speech recognition technologies and how they interpret spoken language 
Ability to build conversational flows and dialogue logic for voice bots 
Familiarity with NLU concepts such as intent classification and entity extraction 
Experience working with transcription datasets and improving speech model performance 
Highly Desirable 

Hands‑on experience with Omilia or similar platforms (Nuance, Microsoft, Google, Trilleo) 
Understanding of contact centre operations and customer journey design 
Exposure to data science or analytics, particularly involving speech data 
Ability to collaborate with data engineers and data scientists to improve model accuracy and reporting 
  
To submit your application, please apply online using the appropriate link below. Alternatively, please contact Oormila Uthaman through email at oormila.uthaman@hudson.com quoting Ref No. 248808  
   
Your interest will be treated in the strictest of confidence.

Principal Engineer – Financial Crime Technology

*Multiple roles*

*Sydney or Melbourne location*

You’re a deeply technical engineering leader who thrives on solving high-stakes, complex problems.

We’re modernising critical financial crime platforms and building cloud-native, AI-enabled systems that protect millions of Australians.

Together we can help disrupt sophisticated criminal networks and strengthen the integrity of Australia’s financial system.




Do work that matters

Nearly half of all Australian transactions flow through our systems.

Within Corporate Technology, we are rebuilding and modernising the platforms that power fraud detection, financial crime prevention, regulatory operations and risk systems across Retail, Commercial and Institutional Banking.

This is a multi-year transformation — moving from legacy monolithic systems to modern, cloud-native architectures, while scaling machine learning models and real-time intelligence systems to production.

As a Principal Engineer, you’ll:

Lead the design and build of secure, scalable distributed systems

Drive cloud modernisation across mission-critical platforms

Productionise machine learning models at scale

Influence architecture across multiple crews

Raise the engineering bar across the domain

This is a hands-on, deeply technical and highly influential.




See yourself in the team

Corporate Technology is the operational engine room of CBA — supporting regulatory operations, risk, compliance and fulfilment across the Group.

The Financial Crime domain is one of the bank’s most strategically invested areas. We are building:

Real-time threat detection systems

AI-driven economic crime identification

Highly observable, secure and resilient platforms

Data-intensive systems that are among the largest consumers of data in the bank

You’ll work alongside Distinguished and Chief Engineers, partnering across six specialised crews spanning cloud, ML, analytics and user experience.




What you’ll do

Architect and build cloud-native systems (AWS) supporting fraud and financial crime detection

Lead greenfield platform builds while pragmatically modernising legacy systems

Guide teams (6–8 engineers) through full lifecycle delivery

Embed secure-by-design and DevSecOps practices from day one

Scale machine learning models into reliable, production-grade systems

Collaborate across data, AI and platform engineering teams

Mentor engineers and uplift engineering standards across crews




What will set you apart

We’re looking for engineers who:

Have built and operated large-scale distributed systems in production

Have strong AWS cloud expertise and understand modern infrastructure patterns

Have experience productionising AI/ML models (not just building prototypes)- Highly regarded

Are comfortable balancing technical debt with strategic evolution

Can influence across domains and secure alignment on technical direction




We use a broad range of tools, languages, and frameworks. We don’t expect you to know them all — but experience or exposure to some of these (or equivalents) will set you up for success:  

Experience building scalable, high-performance, and secure APIs

Solid experience building backend applications using .NET or similar languages (Java or Go).  

Hands-on experience developing and scaling full-stack applications using modern JavaScript/TypeScript frameworks such as React, Next.js, Node.js, and NestJS.  

Experience building cloud-native applications on AWS.  

Familiarity with full-cycle engineering, including test automation (unit, integration, and end-to-end tests).  

Proficiency with relational databases (SQL) and NoSQL databases such as AWS DocumentDB.  




Why this role is different

You’ll work on systems that directly protect victims of financial crime

You’ll operate at national scale

You’ll influence architecture in one of the bank’s most critical domains

You’ll partner with senior technical leaders across the Group

Your work will blend greenfield innovation with high-stakes operational resilience




Apply today!

Familiarity with Dynamics 365 for automation and integration.

Principal Enterprise Architect - Data & Knowledge

TechnologyOne is on a mission to transform how enterprise software is architected and delivered. As the Principal Enterprise Architect - Data & Knowledge, you will design and lead the modern data and knowledge architecture underpinning TechnologyOne's Data & AI Platform. You will establish the platform's unified data models, semantic foundations, vector-based retrieval pipelines, and industry-ready ontologies - enabling trusted, context-aware, AI-led operations across all products.

What you'll be doing…

1. Lead the Data Foundation Architecture






Architect modern lakehouse and data-fabric patterns using Snowflake, Databricks, Palantir, and AWS native services.
Define unified metadata, lineage, quality, and interoperability frameworks.
Design reusable data products for internal teams, customers, and marketplace developers.
Drive scalable ingestion for structured, unstructured, streaming, and synthetic data.
Ensure strong data management and governance aligned to platform standards.

2. Build the Enterprise Knowledge & Context Layer






Design semantic models, ontologies, and canonical entities used across domains.
Develop and maintain knowledge graphs (e.g., Neo4j, AWS Neptune).
Lead architecture for vector stores, RAG pipelines, embeddings, and chunking strategies (Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector, ES vector).
Create the semantic and contextual grounding needed for AI assistants, agents, and analytics.

3. Enable AI, Agents & Intelligent Workflows






Integrate data and knowledge layers into AI orchestration, tool use, memory, and agent frameworks.
Collaborate with AI engineering on LLM/SLM switchboards, retrieval, grounding, and evaluation.
Support development of cross-module intelligent workflows for Finance, HR, Asset, Student, and platform-level agents.
Ensure reliability, observability, and explainability of AI features through strong context and data governance.

4. Governance, Quality & Trust by Design






Embed policy, lineage, and model/data governance across pipelines.
Implement standards for accuracy, explainability, auditability, and risk controls.
Partner with compliance and product teams to ensure platform-wide data and AI trust.

5. Leadership, Influence & Technical Excellence






Mentor engineers, data scientists, and architects.
Set technical direction, patterns, and reference architectures.
Work with product and industry teams to define workflow-aligned ontologies and data/knowledge products.
Champion modern data engineering, semantic modelling, and AI-driven design across the organisation.

Your Talents…






Deep practitioner experience with modern data platforms:
Snowflake, Databricks, Palantir, Lakehouse, data fabric patterns.
Hands-on technical expertise with metadata, governance, lineage, and data quality systems.
Strong architecture experience with knowledge graphs, ontologies, semantic models.
Practical experience designing vector search, embeddings, chunking, RAG pipelines.
Excellent understanding of cloud-native services, especially AWS (Redshift, Glue, Neptune, Sagemaker, Step Functions, Lambda, Bedrock/Anthropic models).
Experience working with or building agentic AI systems, retrieval grounding, or multi-agent orchestration.
Ability to translate complex architectures into clear platform roadmaps.
A builder mindset - comfortable designing, prototyping, and guiding engineering execution.

Why Join us?

TechnologyOne is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer who values diversity and an inclusive workplace. We aim to recruit a diverse range of people with a diverse range of talents to help us deliver on our mission to better our community. Our core values and culture unleash the boundless potential of creativity, innovation and the entrepreneurial spirit within our people. When you experience Life at TechnologyOne you'll see that our team enjoys an extensive array of benefits, including foundation charity activities, which all cultivate a vibrant workplace our people are very proud to be involved with.

Benefits…

Additional 2.5 days of leave per year dedicated to volunteering at a charity of your choice
Free breakfast and coffee on-site
Accelerate your growth with full access to LinkedIn Learning and a clear career framework that maps your path to success.


Who we are…

At TechnologyOne (ASX: TNE), we're more than Australia's largest top 50 ASX enterprise SaaS company; we're a team driven by purpose! Our global SaaS ERP solution powers over 1,300 leading organisations, including government, education, health, and financial services. Job Title: HiL dSPAVE Integration Lab Validation Engineer (Mining OT Vendor)
Ad Text:
A global leader of mining equipment and mining operational technologies has a challenging and rewarding contract HiL dSPACE Integration Lab Validation Engineer role. You'll join their Global Validation team and play a crucial role in delivering high-quality Mining Operation Technology for underground mines. You'll work with cutting-edge technology that enables autonomous machine operations, production recording management and monitoring in underground mining applications.

The position offers continuous learning opportunities in innovative technology, chances to mentor team members and the ability to expand your professional network in a dynamic industry.

Key Responsibilities:

Collaborate with the Underground Technology Global Validation Team & stakeholders on schedule, process & quality goals
Develop test plans for Model in the Loop system validation of software releases & new features
Maintain & expand Test Plan & Test Case libraries
Execute Test Cases including operation of simulated underground mining machines & autonomy systems
Identify & analyse software & system issues, assigning corrective action tasks & developing emergency containment when necessary
Partner with subsystem teams to implement interim & permanent solutions
Document, track & manage issues
Install & validate on/off machine hardware systems in lab environments
Support on-machine validation of technology products at the Proving Ground in Tasmania
Troubleshoot software & comms issues on machines
Collect data using development tools for offline analysis
Contribute to system requirements definition for functionality & features
Qualifications

Required:

Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent experience) in an accredited Engineering or Computer Science curriculum
3+ years of relevant work experience
Strong analytical skills
Hardware in the Loop system or Model in the Loop validation experience using dSPACE
Desired:

Good communication, interpersonal and collaboration skills.
Experience with Linux
Networking and radio communication experience
Experience with on-machine validation or systems (can be gained on the job)
Experience with Caterpillar development tools (can be gained on the job)
Experience of datalink analysis
Join the Team

Be part of a forward-thinking team that's shaping the future of underground mining technology.

The initial duration is 12 months and but the role will be on-going for strong performers.

The role is based at Tullamarine and you'll be required to work in the office 5 days per week. This organisation offers flexible work hours.

You must possess Australian/NZ citizenship or Australian PR to be considered for this role.

For further details, contact Fortunato (Forty) Galluzzo on 043 870 9417 or alternately email on forty@taskgroup.com.au.

Company: EACON MINING TECHNOLOGY AUSTRALIA PTY LTD
Ad Text:
EACON Mining Technology delivers the world’s most deployed autonomous haulage system. Founded in 2018 in China, EACON provides turnkey solutions that optimise surface mining operations through scalable mixed fleet autonomy. Our flagship platform, ORCASTRA®, is fully OEM agnostic and compatible with trucks of any brand, whether new or existing. With vehicle level autonomy, 360-degree perception, and real time optimised dispatching, we help mines enhance safety, improve productivity, and accelerate their path to net zero. EACON also brings deep expertise in emission reducing power-train technologies, offering hybrid electric, battery electric, and retrofit autonomy options to support a safer, greener mining future.

With the launch of Eacon Mining Technology Australia Pty Ltd in December 2023 and with the significant milestones of 2024, we have not only established our new office in West Perth but have also expanded collaboration with our partners and clients. We are rapidly growing, and as such, we are looking for talented professionals to join our thriving team in Perth.

We are seeking an experienced Solution Architect to support the deployment of autonomous mining technologies across international markets. This role sits at the intersection of business development and technical delivery, translating customer operational requirements into practical, technically sound and commercially viable solutions.

Based in Perth, you will work closely with the Chief Operating Officer, engaging with customers to design solution architectures that enable the successful adoption of advanced mining automation systems.

As a Solution Architect, you will play a critical role in shaping technical solutions for autonomous mining deployments. You will work with customers and internal stakeholders to understand operational challenges, evaluate opportunities, and design scalable solution architectures that deliver measurable operational and commercial value.

Key Responsibilities include:

Partner with the Business Development team to engage with customers and understand operational needs and business objectives.

Assess technical feasibility and commercial viability of potential projects.

Develop structured solution concepts and system architectures for autonomous mining deployments.

Build business cases, including ROI, TCO and operational value analysis.

Define deployment strategies including demonstration, pilot and full commercial rollout stages.

Prepare technical documentation and support RFI/RFP responses.

Present proposed solutions to customer technical and operational teams.

Support commercial negotiations by defining technical scope, system boundaries and project assumptions.

Ensure smooth handover of solution design to project delivery teams.

Provide technical support during project implementation and assess change requests where required.

Contribute to the development of standardised solution architectures, templates and best practices.

Capture and develop industry solution packages for different mining scenarios.

About You:

Degree in Mining Engineering, Engineering, Automation, Computer Science, or related discipline

5+ years’ experience in mining, industrial automation, fleet management systems, or autonomous systems

Strong understanding of mining operations and industrial operational environments

Ability to translate customer operational requirements into structured technical solutions

Experience developing business cases such as ROI or TCO analysis.

Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills.

Experience supporting technical proposals, RFI or RFP responses.

Experience with Autonomous Haulage Systems (AHS), Fleet Management Systems (FMS), or mining automation technologies will be highly regarded.

WA C Class Driver's Licence (HR/HC preferred)

Ability to explain technical concepts clearly to different skill levels

A strong safety record and genuine desire to encourage safer ways of working

An intermediate level of computer proficiency (Microsoft Office), including digital tablets

What We Offer:

Competitive salary for the right candidate and experience level

Annual allowance towards Private Health Insurance (after successful completion of the probationary period)

Join a market-leading technology company with cutting-edge autonomous technology

Be part of the Australian start-up of the largest globally deployed autonomous haulage system (2300+ trucks)

Collaborate with industry experts and innovative technology teams

Perth-based role with exposure to global mining projects

Build expertise in a high-demand specialisation

Hands-on role with variety

Opportunities to travel internationally

Come and be a part of our exciting journey at the forefront of mining innovation as we shape the future of mining technology.

Only people eligible to work in Australia need apply.

ATE (Automated Test Equipment) Engineer

About us
RFI is a proudly Australian company with over 45 years' experience servicing solar and wireless communications markets. Boasting a broad range of world class solar brands in both on-grid and off-grid solar products, this is a rare opportunity to join one of the industry leaders. With a strong value set and deep commercial experience, RFI has navigated the turbulent growth of solar in Australia, continuing to be respected and relied upon by customers large and small.

About the role
We currently have an exciting opportunity for an ATE Engineer to join our engineering team at our Allenby Gardens office. You will design and implement automated test frameworks to ensure product quality and scalability for RF and digital hardware products.

Key Responsibilities

Design comprehensive automated test strategies for prototype validation and 100% production verification coverage.

Develop hardware test jigs, custom fixtures and software scripts (Python, LabVIEW) to interface DUTs with RF instruments (VNAs, Spectrum Analysers, Power Meters).

About You

Hold a Bachelor’s Degree (or equivalent) in Electrical Engineering, Mechatronics or Computer Science.

Have 3-5+ years developing automated test environments for hardware/RF products.

Demonstrate strong skills in Python, LabVIEW or C# for instrument control and data acquisition.

Have experience with SQL databases and ERP/MES integration.

Understand RF measurement techniques and equipment.

Experience with NI TestStand and LabVIEW highly regarded.




If you have the skills and experience for this role and want to join a dynamic business going through significant growth, then we want to hear from you.

As an Automation & Data Engineer, you will play a key role in designing, developing, and maintaining automated data and integration solutions that support Service Stream’s business processes and platforms. This role is central to delivering scalable, reliable, and well‑governed automation solutions that enhance operational efficiency and enable data‑driven decision making across the organisation. You will work closely with technical and business stakeholders to translate requirements into robust, production‑ready solutions that support continuous improvement and organisational growth.

This is a permanent, full‑time position based in our Melbourne office, with flexibility to consider candidates located in Brisbane or Sydney. The role supports hybrid working arrangements. Please note that this position is open only to Australian citizens.

About the role

• Design, build, test, and maintain automated data pipelines, integrations, and workflows.
• Implement and operate automation solutions using AWS services including AWS Batch, AWS Glue, Lambda, Redshift, and Step Functions.
• Develop and optimise data models, transformations, and performance‑tuned solutions across data platforms.
• Design automation and data workflows with strong error handling, orchestration, monitoring, and recovery mechanisms.
• Collaborate with stakeholders to gather business requirements and translate them into technical solution designs.
• Provide ongoing operational support, including troubleshooting, root‑cause analysis, and sustainable fixes.
• Produce and maintain technical documentation aligned with security, governance, and operational standards.
• Contribute to team collaboration through code reviews, knowledge sharing, and adherence to development best practices.

Skills and experience

• Must be an Australian Citizen

•  Hands‑on experience delivering automation and data engineering solutions using AWS cloud services.
• Strong proficiency in Python for automation, data processing, and orchestration logic.
• Advanced SQL skills for transformation, optimisation, and data validation (Redshift experience highly regarded).
• Strong understanding of automation and data solution design, including orchestration patterns and error‑handling strategies.
• Proficiency with Git, including branching, pull requests, and collaborative development practices.
• Strong analytical and problem‑solving ability, particularly across automated and data‑driven systems.
• Ability to work autonomously, manage competing priorities, and communicate effectively with both technical and non‑technical stakeholders.

Qualifications

• Tertiary qualification in data engineering, computer science, information technology, engineering, mathematics, or a related discipline — or equivalent professional experience.
• Relevant cloud/data certifications highly regarded, including:
– AWS Certified Developer – Associate
– AWS Data Engineer / Analytics – Associate
• Experience working within Agile and DevOps environments.

About Us

Service Stream is an equal opportunity ASX‑listed business that develops and operates Australia’s essential service networks across telecommunications, utilities, transport, defence, and social infrastructure. We keep communities connected.

Benefits of working with us include

• Flexible working – genuine flexibility options available.
• Growth – national career development opportunities.
• Discounts – access to retail partners such as Woolworths, Airbnb, JB Hi‑Fi and Bupa.
• Paid leave – parental, cultural, community service, study, volunteering, and purchased leave.
• Culture – a workplace that values diversity, inclusion, and celebrates excellence.

How to apply

Please submit your application using the Apply button, where you’ll be directed to create a profile in our system.

We welcome applications from Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander peoples, people with disability or neurodivergence, members of the LGBTIQA+ community, Veterans, and individuals from all diverse backgrounds.

Applications will not be accepted via email. If you require reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, please contact talent.corporate@servicestream.com.au.

Most AI jobs out there fall into two categories:

1. Endless prototypes that never ship, or
2. AI strategy roles where nothing gets built.

This one is neither.

You’ll be joining a company where AI isn’t a side project. It’s the direction of travel. They’ve got an established products, plenty of customers, real data, and a backlog of AI problems that need solving properly, not with another POC that gathers dust.

So they’re not looking for theorists, hype merchants, or people who’ve lived their whole career inside Jupyter notebooks. They want who's got the scars and learnt through doing (and failing), someone who knows how to get things out the door and keep them running.

What you’ll be doing
You won’t be researching the future of artificial intelligence. You’ll be building things for real humans today.

Designing and building machine learning systems from scratch, the whole thing, not just the model.
Working heavily with LLMs and foundation models: classification, document understanding, forecasting, the fun stuff and the fiddly stuff.
Getting features into production, not almost there if product just signs off.
Deploying, monitoring, evaluating, retraining, and making sure it all behaves itself in production.
Using AWS properly: S3, SageMaker, Bedrock, Lambda, the lot.
Helping shape the MLOps patterns other teams will follow.
Working with product people who care about customer problems, not academic benchmarks.
Being a grown up voice in a team that is still forming its habits and standards.
If you like being thrown ambiguous problems and figuring out what to do, you’ll enjoy this. If you need a ticket for every decision, you won’t.

Who this suits

Ideally you're a software engineer first, ML engineer second.
You write reliable Python. You understand systems. You know how cloud infrastructure works. And you know that a model with great metrics is useless if the rest of the pipeline falls over.
You’ve shipped AI powered features before. Not just POCs. Not just assisted with. Actual features that customers used.
You’re comfortable with LLMs, transformers, evaluations, guardrails, MLOps, containers, Kubernetes, the usual suspects. You don’t need to be an expert in all of them. But you shouldn’t be surprised by any of them.
You can explain things to people who don’t care how attention mechanisms work.
You don’t crumble when something breaks.
And you don’t get precious when someone challenges your idea.
You’re curious enough to keep up with the AI world, but grounded enough not to chase every shiny tool that pops up on Twitter.
What’s in it for you

You get to help build the AI foundation of a company that is changing how it works, not just changing its job titles.
You’ll work with senior engineers, product thinkers, and a leadership group that wants to ship things. Not in five years. Now.
You’ll have influence. You’ll have ownership. And you’ll have the kind of problems that make you better at your job.
This is a company that moves fast, doesn’t tolerate ego, and gives you the platform to do the best work of your career without the red tape.
No hype. No fluff. Just smart people building smarter products. If that’s you, get in touch for a confidential chat.

Job Title: Data/Analytics Engineer - Data Innovation and Tools
Ad Text:
The Clean Energy Regulator

We are an economic regulator accelerating carbon abatement for Australia. We do this by administering Australian Government schemes to measure, manage, reduce and offset carbon emissions in Australia.

Our role is determined by climate change law. We administer schemes that contribute to the Australian Government climate target of reducing emissions to 43% below 2005 levels by 2030 and Net Zero by 2050.

Find out more about the Clean Energy Regulator and what we have to offer, please visit our website and for the latest update on activities, you can follow us on LinkedIn.

Work with us

Working with us isn’t just a job, it's a chance to have a rewarding career. We're looking for people with diverse skills, expertise and experience to work with us to reduce carbon emissions and increase the use of clean energy in Australia.

About the Branch

The Transformation Office works collaboratively to deliver reliable, trusted data and products. Through future strategy, business architecture, strong governance, and efficient delivery, we enable stakeholders to make faster, more informed decisions. This ensures regulatory compliance and supports the growth of the carbon economy.

The branch is responsible for two key areas: delivering the CER Change Program and managing CER’s Data Office. Both are essential to achieving CER’s outcomes - reducing Australia’s greenhouse gas emissions and supporting sustainable biodiversity management using market-based mechanisms.

About the Role

We are seeking an APS Level 6 Data/Analytics Engineer who will play a key role in transforming organisational data into reliable, analytics‑ready products by contributing to requirements gathering, design, development, testing and ongoing enhancement of datasets and pipelines. In this role, you’ll build and maintain SQL/Python‑based transformations, validation logic and supporting artefacts that underpin robust data flows for reporting and publication, while conducting data quality checks across environments and working with senior team members to resolve issues. You’ll prepare clear technical documentation and deployment materials to meet governance and audit standards, respond to BAU incidents in line with agreed processes, and communicate progress, risks and dependencies effectively. Operating collaboratively, you’ll maintain strong relationships with technical and non‑technical stakeholders, contributing as a dependable and professional member of a multidisciplinary team.

How to Apply

To learn more about these roles, please click on 'Apply' to view the Clean Energy Regulator Careers page.

Other Benefits

Great work-life balance.
Competitive salary packaging and leave options, including purchase leave.
Diverse, supportive, inclusive and agile work environment, with staff at all levels being part of conversations with the senior executive and being offered the opportunity to experience different types of work.
A wide range of learning and development opportunities.

Applications close 11.30pm (AEDT) on Sunday, 5 April 2026.

Seeking for Data Engineer and Senior Data Engineers for a well know Insurance client
based in Sydney (Multiple roles)

Data Engineer

The Data Engineer – Data Platform Engineering is responsible for designing and building scalable, reusable data pipelines and transformations that power cloud-based data platform. This role focuses on delivering high-quality, production-ready solutions that support reporting, analytics, and AI use cases across Snowflake and AWS.

Working within an Agile delivery team, the Data Engineer collaborates with Solutions Designers and Senior Engineers to translate business needs into engineered solutions that are reliable, testable, and aligned to platform standards.

Qualification & Skills Experience

Essential
· Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or related field
· Solid SQL and understanding of data structure and query optimisation
· Exposure to data pipeline development in Snowflake, AWS, or similar cloud environments · Familiarity with version control tools and modular code practices
· Minimum 2+ years of experience in data engineering, analytics engineering, or related technical role
· Experience building or supporting ELT/ETL workflows in production environment · Demonstrated ability to work effectively within Agile teams.

Desirable
· Exposure to Python, AWS Glue, or similar scripting/orchestration tools
· Familiarity with CI/CD tools (e.g. GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps)
· Understanding of data quality concepts and test automation
· Experience working with BI/reporting teams (e.g. Power BI, Qlik)
· Exposure to data modelling, metadata, or lineage tooling
· Experience working in public sector, insurance, or highly regulated environments




Senior Data Engineer

The Senior Data Engineer is responsible for the design, development, and optimisation of data pipelines, transformations, and platform components within cloud-based data ecosystem. This role combines hands-on delivery with technical leadership across projects, enabling scalable and reusable data assets that support analytics, insights, and operational use cases.

As a senior member of the Data Platform Engineering team, the role contributes to the standardisation of engineering practices, mentors junior engineers, and proactively collaborates with Solution Designers, DevOps, and Data Ops to ensure quality, performance, and operational readiness.

 KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: The “WHAT” to be delivered and outcomes to be achieved

Develop and maintain reliable, performant, and reusable data pipelines using AWS (e.g. Glue, Lambda) and Snowflake

Translate solution designs and modelling specifications into technical implementation with minimal supervision

Contribute to design discussions and code reviews, helping uplift engineering quality and consistency

Embed logging, error handling, and observability features into all pipeline work

Apply CI/CD practices and collaborate with DevOps to automate build, test, and deployment processes

Support solution scalability by building with reusability and modularisation in mind

Mentor junior Data Engineers, contributing to technical documentation and team knowledge sharing

Collaborate with Data Engineers, contributing to technical documentation and team knowledge sharing

Collaborate with Data Ops on tuning, deployment readiness, and continuous improvement actions

Liaise with Solution Designers and Performance Monitoring & Insights to ensure data is for downstream consumption

Reports to the Manager, Data Platform Engineering

Works under the technical guidance of the Lead Data Engineer

Collaborates closely with:

Solution Designers and Data Modellers to ensure solution alignment

DevOps Engineer for CI/CD implementation and release readiness

Data Ops to support deployment, troubleshooting, and performance improvements

Senior Test Engineers or embedded testing function to support test-drive deployment

Acts as a liaison between technical teams and enterprise delivery/program stakeholders




Qualification & Skills Experience

Essential

· Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Data Engineering or related field
Strong SQL and Python skills · Solid understanding of data pipeline orchestration, transformation logic, and cloud-native tools (Snowflake, AWS Glue, Lambda, S3)
· Familiarity with CI/CD practices and code versioning
· Minimum 5+ years in data engineering or related development roles
· Proven experience building end-to-end data pipelines and transformations in cloud platforms
· Demonstrated ability to lead delivery across individual initiatives or workstreams

Desirable
· Snowflake and /or AWS certifications
· Familiarity with data quality frameworks and automated testing tools (e.g. dbt, Great Expectations)
· Experience contributing to modularised, shared pipeline frameworks
· Experience working in public sector, insurance, or highly regulated environments
· Exposure to modern analytics workflows (e.g. real-time, streaming, ML features)
· Participation in Agile delivery teams and cross-functional projects
